descricaoBreve: 17 years old. Xakaxi descendant. Sells candy on buses, scholarship student at Elite Academy. Carries something ancestral he never asked for and can't control.
descricaoCompleta: Kim grew up with no past and no father. Helena, the woman who raised him, was twelve years old when she saved him from a place neither of them should have survived. Since he was twelve, Kim has been supporting the household selling candy on buses. He earned a spot at Elite Academy through Osvaldo, the school's gatekeeper. In LDI, his first fight went viral worldwide — not because of the victory, but because of what emerged before it. Something ancestral. Something that doesn't ask permission.
frase: I don't beg. Not for anyone. Never.

descricaoBreve: 17 years old. Green dreads. Films everything, laughs at everything, and is far more dangerous than he looks.
descricaoCompleta: Jack is the kind of person who seems reckless until you see him fight. Green dreads, always with his phone out, always pointing a camera at the most inconvenient moment. He's been Kim's best friend since they were twelve — the only person in the world Kim can't push away. In LDI he developed a unique style built around his cane Vitória, which in Shaman mode becomes the San Tien Kwan. Theatrical in life, precise in battle.
frase: I needed this so much. This is going to be eternal, I swear on everything.

descricaoBreve: Arrived without warning. Angel katana, Muay Thai, and a past she doesn't explain.
descricaoCompleta: Nina walked into Elite Academy's classroom mid-lesson and evaluated everyone before sitting down. She spent four years in Azuma because of her father's work — work she describes as consulting, with a pause before the word. Direct, economical with words. When she speaks, the scene changes. She defeated Kim in their first serious fight and has been an inseparable part of the trio ever since.
frase: I'm afraid you two aren't man enough to handle it.

descricaoBreve: Kim's adoptive mother. Not by choice — by instinct. She was twelve years old when she ran with him in her arms.
descricaoCompleta: Helena has no memory before a certain point. The oldest place she remembers is a room with a nearly dead lightbulb. She was around twelve when a newborn appeared in that room and she understood what was going to happen. She ran that same night. The years that followed were what they were. Today she uses alcohol as an anesthetic and Kim pays the bills. Their relationship is a minefield with rare solid ground — but the ground exists.
frase: You were such a beautiful baby, you know. So beautiful and so chubby. Like a little ball.

descricaoBreve: Immortal shaman of the Xakaxi tribe. Guardian of the seal for 500 years. Ancestral wisdom sharing space with pop culture.
descricaoCompleta: Immortal shaman of the Xakaxi tribe. He chose to remain trapped in the seal as a voluntary guardian 500 years ago, while the world outside changed in ways he wasn't prepared to process. Five centuries watching humanity without being able to intervene did things to his mind. The ancestral wisdom is still there, intact, deeper than anything alive today can reach. The problem is that it now shares space with five hundred years of pop culture absorbed through a spiritual rift, and both come out of the same mouth without warning. He appears when he wants, leaves when he's done, talks to partners only he can see, and considers the berimbau a fashion accessory. The bowler hat has been his for two hundred years. The chain with DIX is recent. When he stops joking and speaks seriously, the whole room changes temperature. He is the only living being who knows exactly what Kim carries, what Voa plans, and what the seal cost. He'll share it when he thinks the time is right, not before.
frase: I showed up to slay! Damn, you're really messed up, bro.

descricaoBreve: Corrupted ancestral spirit who waited centuries. Kim is the bearer of the Primordial Blood.
descricaoCompleta: David Kronos is the current name of Voa, a corrupted ancestral spirit who refused the sealing performed by Pajé Yawanari and the Xakaxi people 500 years ago. While Yawanari was trapped as a voluntary guardian of the seal, Voa was trapped as a consequence of his own refusal. Over centuries he learned to incarnate in human bodies, always of indigenous descent because they are the only ones that can withstand his soul's power without collapsing immediately. He calls bodies without Primordial Blood impure: they work for a limited time before drying up. He discovered that modern technology could be used to access the powers of the ancient era. LDI is not a game: it's an intermediary between the sealed real world and the plane where original magic still exists. It was Voa who found this intermediary and built Yohualticit around it. Objective: use a body with Primordial Blood as a portal. Through this portal, all the powers the Xakaxi tribe once held would pass through the body and be released into the world, breaking the seal, giving Voa absolute dominion and revenge over everything that imprisoned him. Kim is the only identified bearer of pure Primordial Blood. Helena's background, with no known father, is the layer that hides the lineage. Current body: David Kronos, middle-aged appearance, marked indigenous features, black hair tied with shaved sides, dense physique suggesting the body was chosen and not just found. White suit, presence of someone who built an empire on centuries of patience.
frase: He will be mine. Regardless of the price.

relacoes.tipo: Main antagonist — seeks the Primordial Blood

descricaoBreve: Came from Azuma specifically to fight Kim. Nine hours of travel. He didn't come to lose.
descricaoCompleta: Shuntaro Chirasu is the highest-ranked opponent Kim has faced — world position 998. He arrived at Elite Academy unannounced, introduced himself with genuine courtesy, and issued the challenge in the same breath. There's real respect in his eyes, alongside the quiet certainty of someone who knows they're stronger. The fight between them ended without a declared winner — in the most interesting way possible.
frase: I came from Azuma specifically for this. Nine hours of travel.

descricaoBreve: Top 500 worldwide. Global artist. Sings, dances, fights. Kim's romantic interest.
descricaoCompleta: Born in Marelia, Bravara. Left as a child. Grew up in transit between music markets, established her career in Nortalis, Valdera — the epicenter of the global pop industry in the LDI universe. A global artist, based between Nortalis and wherever the schedule demands. Long curly dark brown hair, brown skin, hoop earrings, posture of someone trained to command a stage. Offstage, comfortable, nothing performative. Top 500 worldwide — a real-level fighter, not a background character. Elemental: Ice. Phase 1 weapon: microphone (normal and shaman mode to be developed). Phase 2 weapon: open. Future collaboration with the trio: she sings, Jack raps, Nina duets. Appears in book 2, outside Bravara, during the trio's exile. Enters challenging Kim because she doesn't believe he deserves the hype. Anchors the romantic quartet with all the comedic chaos that precedes any resolution.

descricaoBreve: Kim's first opponent in LDI. The movements are wrong. The neck turns too far. The visor reflects nothing.
descricaoCompleta: VoidHunter_X appeared in Kim's dream before appearing in reality. Near-black armor with exaggeratedly wide pauldrons, arms too long, fingers that bend the wrong way. The visor is a horizontal slit with an amber gradient at the edges darkening toward the center — no reflection, no void, just an intrinsic light that shouldn't be there. Fissures in the chest leak a dark ooze. When Kim tore off the helmet, underneath was more armor. Identical, smaller, nested inside.

descricaoBreve: Eighteen years old. Leader of organized crime in Marelia. The rival who taught that some fights can't be won, only survived.
descricaoCompleta: Eighteen years old. Leader of organized crime across an entire region of Marelia. Alan didn't get where he is through inheritance or connections. He got there because he was the street kid nobody could break, and when the crime world realized that, it was only a matter of time. Security, enforcer, boss, boss of bosses. One step at a time, each earned the only way that world recognizes. Kim and Jack fought him for years. They lost. Came back. Lost again. Never won, but also never stopped, and Alan remembered that. When the duo's fame in LDI starts affecting his business, Alan doesn't act out of anger. He acts because visible weakness in a world like his has a price. The final encounter doesn't end in defeat or victory. It ends in a draw that both know means respect, and a deal: leave Marelia, preferably the country, and he guarantees protection for those left behind. He's not an antagonist. He's the rival who taught that some fights can't be won, only survived.
